_meta.description: IRC §127 employer educational-assistance / student-loan-repayment tax-benefit parameters for tax year 2026. Drives /employer-student-loan-repayment-calculator/. The $5,250 exclusion was made PERMANENT and inflation-indexed (2027+) by OBBBA / P.L. 119-21 §70412, effective for payments made after 12/31/2025 (26 U.S.C. §127(a)(2), (c)(1)(B), (d)). Dual employee exclusion from income-tax withholding (§3401(a)(18)), FICA (§3121(a)(18)) and FUTA (§3306(b)(13)). STANDALONE dataset: this is an EXCLUSION, not an OBBBA below-the-line deduction, so it deliberately shares no engine or data file with obbba-deductions-2026.json / obbba-deduction.js.
_meta.confidence: HIGH on all statutory mechanics (verbatim Cornell text): $5,250 cap, shared cap, dual exclusion, 7.65% two-sided FICA, nearest-$50 indexing on the INCREASE, first indexed year 2027, base year 2025. HIGH on the 2026 Social Security wage base $184,500 (SSA press release 2025-10-24). The 2027 indexed cap does NOT exist yet (official2027Cap: null) — the engine fails closed to $5,250 until the IRS annual inflation-adjustment Rev. Proc. (expected fall 2026) is published. OBBBA section number §70412 is HIGH-minus (corroborated by BDO/Mercer/Crowell/EBEC + the Cornell P.L. 119-21 amendment note; enrolled-statute PDF not itself fetched).
_meta.notImplementedByDesign: FUTA (§3306(b)(13)) is excluded too but adds ~$0: the $7,000 FUTA base is already exhausted for essentially every benefit-receiving employee, so the marginal FUTA saving is zero — it is intentionally NOT in the headline (spec §1.6). State unemployment (SUTA) wage bases vary and are out of scope.

indexing.roundingRule: The INCREASE (not the total) rounds to the NEAREST multiple of $50 and can round UP — IRC §127(d)(2), which diverges from the usual round-down COLA pattern.

sources.claim: $5,250 exclusion + shared cap + inflation indexing (nearest $50, 2027+) + loan-payment clause made permanent
Income-tax withholding wage exclusion for §127 amounts
IRS FAQ (FS-2026-10, April 2026): combined-cap language + 'adjusted for increases in the cost of living for taxable years beginning after 2026' + sample plan document
Pub 15-B (2026): over-$5,250 amounts are wages; fringe-benefit exclusion
California non-conformity: employer loan repayment is taxable CA wages; AB 386 failed 2026-02-02
